response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine Staff-level experience, a strong TypeScript background, and directly relevant hands-on work with agentic systems and MCP infrastructure in his current role. His time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second demonstrates real distributed-systems depth. However, the role explicitly requires strong Python, which is entirely absent from both the resume narrative and the skills list — a material gap against a stated must-have. Evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, formal incident response, and on-call ownership is also thin, making it worth a screening call to probe those areas before committing to a full loop.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience: low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of req/sec with async I/O and high-performance caching
Current hands-on AI/agentic infrastructure work: MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq, directly matching the AI-native environment expectation
Repeated Staff-level tenure across multiple companies with evidence of technical strategy, devops ownership, and community knowledge-sharing
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — skills list, role descriptions, and projects — despite being listed as a must-have in the job description
Limited evidence of stakeholder management across engineering, product, and leadership functions or formal incident-response and on-call ownership
High-throughput systems work is concentrated in 2018–2020; more recent roles (Optilyz, RIDE) are smaller-scale product engineering with less systems depth
